			<p>A professional invoice looks like a one-page document containing nine required fields: invoice number, business and customer details, issue and due dates, itemised goods or services, subtotal, applicable taxes, total amount due, and payment terms — laid out clearly with the total prominently displayed.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">In the United States, the IRS requires “adequate records” but no specific format. In the United Kingdom and the European Union, VAT invoices must follow specific field rules. In Australia, GST-registered businesses must label the document “Tax invoice” and show their ABN. The fields are universal — labels and tax handling vary by country.</span></p>

<h2><span id="What_does_a_professional_invoice_look_like"><b>What does a professional invoice look like?</b></span></h2>
<p>A professional invoice is a structured one-page document that requests payment for goods or services delivered, identifies both parties, itemises what is being billed, and states the total amount due along with the payment terms.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">It differs from a basic invoice in three ways: a consistent layout an accounts-payable team can scan in seconds, the legal information required in the seller’s jurisdiction, and no ambiguity about what is owed, when, and how to pay it. Use a </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/invoice-template/">free invoice template</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">if you want the structure pre-built — the country-specific differences are explained below.</span></p>
<h2><span id="Why_a_professional_invoice_matters"><b>Why a professional invoice matters</b></span></h2>
<p>A professional invoice accelerates payment, protects the seller during tax audits, defends the business in disputes, and signals operational maturity to clients.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Accounts-payable teams in mid-size and large companies process hundreds of invoices a week. Anything that slows them down — missing PO number, ambiguous total, vague description — moves the invoice to a manual review queue. Combine clean formatting with clear </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/understanding-invoice-payment-terms/">invoice payment terms</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">and </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/features/payment-reminders/">automatic payment reminders</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">and the time from issue to paid drops noticeably.</span></p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Tax authorities in the US, UK, EU and Australia all expect invoices to evidence claimed income. Missing or duplicate invoice numbers are flagged in audits. If a customer disputes the amount, the invoice is the primary evidence — a complete, itemised invoice with all the </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/advice/essential-invoice-elements/">essential invoice elements</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">is a legally binding document; an ad-hoc one is not. The same standard applies whether you issue freelance invoices, retainers, or any of the </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/types-of-invoices/">types of invoices</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">your business uses.</span></p>
<h2><span id="9_elements_every_professional_invoice_must_include"><b>9 elements every professional invoice must include</b></span></h2>
<p>A professional invoice contains nine required fields: a unique invoice number, your business details, the customer’s details, the issue date and due date, an itemised list of goods or services, the subtotal, applicable taxes, the total amount due, and the payment terms.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Country-specific labels and a few additional requirements (VAT registration, ABN) layer on top.</span></p>
<h3><span id="1_Invoice_number"><b>1. Invoice number</b></span></h3>
<p>Every professional invoice carries a unique invoice number — typically a sequential alphanumeric identifier such as INV-2026-0001 — placed in the top-right of the invoice header.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">UK and EU rules require sequential numbering with no gaps. The IRS and ATO require uniqueness but not strict sequence. See </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/what-is-an-invoice-number/">invoice number format and examples</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">for the full conventions.</span></p>
<h3><span id="2_Your_business_details"><b>2. Your business details</b></span></h3>
<p>The seller’s business name, registered address, contact details, and tax or company registration number appear at the top of the invoice.</p>
<h3><span id="3_Customer_details"><b>3. Customer details</b></span></h3>
<p>The customer’s legal business name, billing address, and (for B2B invoices) tax registration number appear directly below the seller’s details, labelled “Bill To”.</p>
<h3><span id="4_Issue_date_and_due_date"><b>4. Issue date and due date</b></span></h3>
<p>The issue date marks when the invoice was created and the due date marks when payment is required — both appear in the header block.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">UK VAT rules require an invoice within 30 days of supply; the ATO requires Australian tax invoices within 28 days of customer request. </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/features/invoice-tracking/">Invoice tracking</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">with these dates makes reconciliation straightforward.</span></p>
<h3><span id="5_Logo_and_brand_identity"><b>5. Logo and brand identity</b></span></h3>
<p>A logo and consistent visual identity appear in the header to confirm the seller’s identity at a glance and reinforce brand trust.</p>
<h3><span id="6_Itemised_line_items"><b>6. Itemised line items</b></span></h3>
<p>Each good or service is listed on a separate line with a clear description, quantity, unit price, and line total — typically in a table in the middle of the invoice.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Vague descriptions are the most common reason B2B accounts-payable teams reject invoices. “Strategy workshop, 4 hours, 12 March 2026” is specific. “Consulting services” is not.</span></p>
<h3><span id="7_Subtotal_taxes_and_total"><b>7. Subtotal, taxes, and total</b></span></h3>
<p>The subtotal, applicable taxes, and grand total appear stacked at the bottom-right, with the total displayed in the largest, most prominent typography.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Tax must be itemised separately on VAT and GST invoices. If you </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/features/accept-payments-online/">accept payments online</a></strong><span style="font-weight: 400;">, placing a payment-link button beside the total reduces friction further.</span></p>
<h3><span id="8_Payment_terms_and_methods"><b>8. Payment terms and methods</b></span></h3>
<p>The payment terms — including the payment window, accepted payment methods, and any late-payment penalties — appear in a fine-print section below the totals.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">“Payment due within 30 days. Late payments incur 1.5% monthly interest. Pay by bank transfer or card.” See our guide on </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/selecting-the-right-payment-methods/">selecting the right payment methods</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">for which options serve which client types.</span></p>
<h3><span id="9_Thank-you_note_or_signature_block"><b>9. Thank-you note or signature block</b></span></h3>
<p>A short thank-you note or signature appears at the bottom of the invoice as the closing professional touch.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Optional but high-value. The cover email matters too — see </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/how-to-write-an-effective-invoice-email/">how to write an effective invoice email</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">for the framing that gets invoices opened and processed.</span></p>
<h2><span id="Country-by-country_invoice_requirements"><b>Country-by-country invoice requirements</b></span></h2>
<p>Invoice rules are set by each country’s tax authority: the IRS in the US requires adequate records, HMRC in the UK mandates specific VAT-invoice fields, the ATO in Australia requires GST-invoice formatting, and the EU VAT Directive sets minimum fields for every member state.</p>
<h3><span id="United_States_-_IRS"><b>United States — IRS</b></span></h3>
<p>US federal law does not prescribe an invoice format, but the IRS requires businesses to maintain adequate records that substantiate all reported income.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Per IRS Publication 583, every invoice must have a unique identifier, an issue date, both parties’ details, itemised charges, and a total. Sequential numbering is recommended, not required. Records must be kept at least 3 years. Sales tax is set at state and local level — there is no federal sales tax. Electronic invoices are fully accepted.</span></p>
<h3><span id="United_Kingdom_-_HMRC"><b>United Kingdom — HMRC</b></span></h3>
<p>HMRC requires UK VAT-registered businesses to issue invoices with a unique sequential invoice number, both parties’ VAT details, the supply date, an itemised description, and VAT broken out separately at each rate.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Under VAT Notice 700/21, a UK VAT invoice must show a unique sequential number with no gaps, the seller’s VAT number, the customer’s name and address, the issue and supply dates, line-item descriptions and unit prices, the VAT rate per line, the VAT total in Sterling, and the gross total. Simplified VAT invoices are allowed for retail sales of £250 or less. Invoices must be issued within 30 days of supply and kept for at least 6 years.</span></p>
<h3><span id="Australia_-_ATO"><b>Australia — ATO</b></span></h3>
<p>The Australian Taxation Office requires every tax invoice to be labelled “Tax invoice”, show the seller’s ABN, identify both parties, and itemise the GST charged on each supply.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Per GSTR 2013/1, GST-registered businesses must issue tax invoices within 28 days of customer request for any taxable sale of AUD 82.50 or more (including GST). For sales of AUD 1,000+, the buyer’s identity or ABN must also appear. Tax invoices must be retained for at least 5 years. Australian businesses are increasingly adopting Peppol e-invoicing for B2B and B2G.</span></p>
<h3><span id="European_Union_-_VAT_Directive"><b>European Union — VAT Directive</b></span></h3>
<p>The EU VAT Directive requires every invoice issued in a member state to contain a sequential number that uniquely identifies the invoice, both parties’ VAT details, the supply date, the taxable amount, and the VAT rate and amount per rate.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">Article 226 of Council Directive 2006/112/EC sets the minimum invoice fields. Member states may add their own requirements — Germany’s GoBD rules require tamper-proof numbering, for example. Most member states require invoices retained 6–10 years. The EU is rolling out mandatory </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/electronic-invoice/">electronic invoicing</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">or B2B supplies under the ViDA initiative through 2030.</span></p>

<h2><span id="How_to_design_a_professional_invoice"><b>How to design a professional invoice</b></span></h2>
<p>A professional invoice is designed for scannability: a clear visual hierarchy with the total prominent, branded but uncluttered, sized for both screen and A4/Letter print.</p>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The buyer’s AP team needs to find five things in three seconds: who the invoice is from, what it is for, the total, the due date, and how to pay. Four design principles deliver this:</span></p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Lead with the total. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Largest type, contrasting weight, positioned where the eye lands first.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Brand without clutter. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Logo small enough not to compete with the content; brand colours accent rather than flood; consistent typography.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Mobile-readable, print-friendly. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Most clients read on phone first. Single-column layout, generous spacing, fits on one A4 / US Letter page when printed. Always export as PDF.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Cut what is not needed. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">No marketing copy, no decorative borders, no mid-document promotions.</span></li>
</ul>
<p><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you start from a </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/invoice-template/">free invoice template</a></strong><span style="font-weight: 400;">, these principles are built in. The full process from blank to send-ready is in our guide on </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/how-to-make-an-invoice/">how to make an invoice</a></strong><span style="font-weight: 400;">, and the cover email is covered in </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/how-to-send-an-invoice/">how to send an invoice</a></strong><span style="font-weight: 400;">.</span></p>
<h2><span id="5_reasons_B2B_accounts_payable_rejects_invoices"><b>5 reasons B2B accounts payable rejects invoices</b></span></h2>
<p>B2B accounts-payable teams reject invoices for five recurring reasons: missing or wrong invoice number, wrong tax treatment, vague itemisation, mismatched purchase-order reference, and wrong “Bill To” entity.</p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Invoice number is missing, wrong, or duplicates an earlier one. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">AP software flags duplicates automatically. Use a </span><strong><a href="https://www.billdu.com/blog/what-is-an-invoice-number/">consistent invoice number format</a></strong> <span style="font-weight: 400;">and never reuse a number, even across years.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Tax treatment is wrong or registration is missing. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">VAT-registered UK and EU sellers who omit their VAT number fail compliance. Australian sellers who forget to label “Tax invoice” or omit their ABN fail compliance.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Line items are vague. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">AP teams need enough description to match the invoice to the contract or PO. Use date, deliverable, quantity, and unit rate.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Purchase-order number is missing or doesn’t match. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">In any company that uses POs, the PO number is the primary key for matching invoice to authorised spend. One wrong digit can stall payment by weeks.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>“Bill To” entity doesn’t match the legal customer. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Invoices addressed to an individual contact, a project name, or a subsidiary that isn’t the contracting party fail compliance. Confirm the legal entity before sending the first invoice.</span></li>
</ul>
<h2><span id="6_common_professional_invoice_mistakes"><b>6 common professional invoice mistakes</b></span></h2>
<p>The six most common professional-invoice mistakes are burying the total, inconsistent numbering, missing payment terms, mid-invoice marketing copy, no “Invoice” label, and ambiguous currency notation.</p>
<ul>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Burying the total. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">If the total is the same size as the line items, the buyer has to read the whole invoice to find what they owe.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Inconsistent numbering. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Switching format mid-year, skipping numbers, or restarting without a year prefix breaks audit trails.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Missing payment terms. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Without explicit terms, the invoice is harder to enforce and pushes the average days-to-pay upward.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Mid-invoice marketing copy. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">“Try our new product!” belongs in your cover email, not your invoice.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>No “Invoice” label. </b><span style="font-weight: 400;">Quotes, estimates, and proforma documents look similar from a distance and routinely get confused.</span></li>
<li style="font-weight: 400;" aria-level="1"><b>Ambiguous currency notation. </b>“$” alone is ambiguous between USD, AUD, CAD, NZD. Use the ISO code (USD, AUD) or the explicit currency name.</li>
</ul>

						<div class="col-12 accordion-group js-accordion-group p--margin-1 p--size-20 p--sm-size-16">
							
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item is-active ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						What does a professional invoice look like?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						<p>A professional invoice is a one-page document with a clear header, structured itemisation, and nine required fields: invoice number, your business details, the customer's details, issue and due dates, itemised goods or services, subtotal, taxes, total, and payment terms.</p>
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						What are the 9 essential elements of a professional invoice?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						The nine essential elements are a unique invoice number, your business details, the customer's details, the issue date and due date, an itemised list of goods or services, the subtotal, applicable taxes, the total amount due, and the payment terms.
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						What does a UK VAT invoice look like?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						<p>A UK VAT invoice contains a unique sequential invoice number, the seller's VAT registration number, the customer's name and address, the invoice and supply dates, an itemised description, the VAT rate per line, the VAT amount, and the gross total.</p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">The full requirements are in HMRC VAT Notice 700/21. Simplified VAT invoices are allowed for retail sales of £250 or less.</span></p>
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						What does an Australian tax invoice look like?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						<p>An Australian tax invoice carries the words "Tax invoice", the seller's ABN, the invoice date, an itemised description with quantity and price, and the GST amount displayed separately.</p><p><span style="font-weight: 400;">For sales of AUD 1,000 or more, the buyer's identity or ABN must also appear. Format set out in GSTR 2013/1.</span></p>
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						Does a US invoice need a specific format?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						US federal law does not mandate an invoice format, but the IRS requires every business to keep adequate records of income — every invoice must have a unique identifier, an issue date, both parties' details, itemised charges, and a total.
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						Is a logo required on a professional invoice?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						A logo is not legally required on any invoice in the US, UK, EU, or Australia, but it is strongly recommended because it confirms the seller's identity at a glance and reduces fraud risk.
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						Do invoices need a signature?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						A signature is not legally required on most invoices, but having the buyer sign and return the invoice as confirmation strengthens the document if a dispute arises later.
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						What's the best file format for a professional invoice?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						PDF is the preferred format for professional invoices because it preserves layout across devices, prints reliably, and cannot be silently edited.
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						How long should a professional invoice be?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						A professional invoice should fit on a single A4 or US Letter page wherever possible, with multi-page invoices reserved for projects with extensive line-item detail.
					</div>
				</div>
				<div class="accordion__item js-accordion-item  ">
					<div class="d-flex accordion__trigger js-accordion-trigger">
						
						Do I need different invoice templates for different countries?
					</div>
					<div class="accordion__content js-accordion-content">
						If you sell across borders, you typically need country-specific invoice variants because tax rules, required fields, and label conventions differ between the US, UK, EU, and Australia.
					</div>
				</div>

<script type="text/rocketlazyloadscript">
    (function(){

        // Helper: open modal with file url
        const modal = document.getElementById('downloadEmailModal');
        const modalCloseBtn = modal.querySelector('.download-modal-close');
        const form = document.getElementById('downloadEmailForm');
        const emailInput = document.getElementById('downloadEmailInput');
        const fileInput = document.getElementById('downloadFileInput');
        // const cancelBtn = document.getElementById('downloadCancelBtn');
        const msg = document.getElementById('downloadMsg');

        let __billdu_prev_active = null;

        function openModal(fileUrl){
            // save previously focused element to restore later
            try { __billdu_prev_active = document.activeElement; } catch(e) { __billdu_prev_active = null; }
            fileInput.value = fileUrl;
            emailInput.value = '';
            msg.style.display = 'none';
            modal.style.display = 'flex';
            modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden','false');
            // move focus into the email input for accessibility
            setTimeout(function(){ try{ emailInput.focus(); }catch(e){} }, 10);
        }

        function closeModal(){
            modal.style.display = 'none';
            modal.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true');
        }

        function closeModalAndClear(){
            // clear interception flag so other scripts behave normally
            try { window.__billdu_download_intercepted = false; } catch(e){}
            closeModal();
            // restore previous focus
            try { if(__billdu_prev_active && typeof __billdu_prev_active.focus === 'function') __billdu_prev_active.focus(); } catch(e){}
            __billdu_prev_active = null;
        }

        // Close button handler: clear interception flag and close modal
        if (modalCloseBtn) {
            modalCloseBtn.addEventListener('click', function(e){
                e.preventDefault();
                closeModalAndClear();
            });
        }

        // cancelBtn.addEventListener('click', function(e){ e.preventDefault(); closeModal(); });

        // Close when clicking on the overlay (outside the modal-inner)
        modal.addEventListener('click', function(e){
            if (e.target === modal) {
                closeModalAndClear();
            }
        });

        // Close on Escape key
        document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e){
            if (e.key === 'Escape' || e.key === 'Esc') {
                if (modal.getAttribute('aria-hidden') === 'false') {
                    closeModalAndClear();
                }
            }
        });

        // Use event delegation so links injected later (Elementor widgets) are caught
        document.addEventListener('click', function(e){

            // Respect modifier keys and middle clicks
            if (e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey || e.shiftKey || e.button === 1) return;

            const a = e.target.closest('a');
            if (!a) return;

            const href = a.href || '';
            // Patterns to intercept: specific IDs, uploads docx/xlsx, or Google Sheets
            const isIdTarget = e.target.getAttribute('data-id') === 'word_download' || e.target.getAttribute('data-id') === 'excel_download' || e.target.getAttribute('data-id') === 'google_sheet_download';
            const idButton = e.target.getAttribute('data-id');
            const isUploadFile = href.includes('/wp-content/uploads/') && (href.match(/\.docx$/i) || href.match(/\.xlsx$/i));
            const isGoogleSheet = href.includes('docs.google.com/spreadsheets');

            console.log(idButton);

            if (isIdTarget || isUploadFile || isGoogleSheet) {
                // Don't intercept if the link has download attribute set to force direct download or explicitly opted out
                if (a.hasAttribute('data-no-intercept')) return;

                // Prevent default navigation and stop other click handlers (prevents page reload from other listeners)
                e.preventDefault();
                try{
                    e.stopImmediatePropagation();
                } catch(err){}
                e.stopPropagation();

                // Mark that we intercepted this click so other scripts (data-layer) can skip reload
                try { window.__billdu_download_intercepted = true; } catch(err){}

                const submitBtn = document.querySelector('button.button-popup');
                if (submitBtn && idButton) {
                    submitBtn.setAttribute('id', idButton);
                }

                openModal(href);
            }
        }, true); // use capture to catch early

        // Submit handler: send email+file to server, then trigger download
        form.addEventListener('submit', function(e){
            e.preventDefault();
            const email = emailInput.value.trim();
            const file = fileInput.value.trim();
            if(!email){ msg.textContent = 'Please enter a valid email.'; msg.style.display = 'block'; return; }
            const submitBtn = document.querySelector('button.button-popup');
            submitBtn.disabled = true;
            msg.style.display = 'none';

            // Prepare POST to admin-ajax
            const fd = new FormData();
            fd.append('action', 'record_download_email');
            fd.append('email', email);
            fd.append('file', file);

            fetch('https://www.billdu.com/wordpress/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php', {
                method: 'POST',
                credentials: 'same-origin',
                body: fd
            }).then(r => r.json()).then(data => {
                submitBtn.disabled = false;
                if(!data || (!data.success && !data.data)){
                    msg.textContent = data && data.data ? data.data : 'Error saving email';
                    msg.style.display = 'block';
                    return;
                }
                const downloadUrl = (data.success && data.data && data.data.download_url) ? data.data.download_url : (data.download_url || file);
                // If Google Docs/Sheets, redirect instead of download
                closeModalAndClear();
                window.location.href = downloadUrl;
                return;
            }).catch(err => {
                submitBtn.disabled = false;
                msg.textContent = 'Network error';
                msg.style.display = 'block';
                console.error(err);
            });
         });
     })();

</script>
